probable economic outcomes irrespective
of what our signals are currently
signaling today so that when the signals
change because again kiss and Dr Mo are

management overlays when those signals

unbalances about two to three uh uh RoR
R phase transitions in the market regime
every year that means going from a risk
on regime to a risk-off regime or from a
risk-off regime to a risk on regime and
that's when you need to do something
materially different in your portfolio
to remain on the right side of Market
risk and you know obviously in most
years that means you're talking about
one 10% correction so going down into it
coming back out of it and potentially
going down into another one so that's
that's three RoR phase transitions right
there or if you're in a bare Market
you're talking about a significant U um
a reversal a significant rally in a bare
Market going back into the sorry during
a bare Market going back to the bare
market and then another significant
rally so that's typically how the path
that asset markets take uh every year
you can sort of look through that if you
want but the reality is if you look
through that and it turns out to be not
what you expected xanti that's when you
start to blow up your portfolio and
expose yourself to fomo or Foo fomo fear

tight easy financial conditions

they function and how they track now so
um you know so the number one way to to
see if an economy is sort of you know
approaching stall speed is obviously you
know is clearly where you are in the
labor market cycle you know obviously
the unemployment rate is not going to go
to zero or or negative and so ultimately
you're going to wind up with a
structurally low level of an unemploy
in an econ in a fully employed economy
that just you know you won't be able to
get below that level doesn't mean it has
to go up immediately once you get to
that point but obviously you know you're
not going to continue to decline from
that level uh but one other way
economists um you know kind of think
about an economy operating above
potential is is looking through the
output gap which is the deviation from a
trend potential GDP now that's a loaded
statement and of itself because
potential GDP uh you know is is there
are there are you know models on on on
uh in academic circles that uh try to to
uh uh you know sort of um calculate
potential GDP which is the sort of
potential growth rate of the economy uh
in the absence of you know sort of
cyclical influences and the reality is
you know potential GDP changes all the
time and so there's even if you had a
model's estimate of potential GDP we can
see how much above or below uh uh that
Trend based estimate of potential GDP is
at any given time and we're obviously
oper well not obviously obviously is a
strong word in this context we may be
operating above potential at least
According to some of the Legacy models
of potential GDP CB publish has W I want
to say the FED has one as well um there
are many models but the reality is um
you if you have a positive output Gap
I.E we're growing above potential then
you you can make the case that the
econom is probably du to slow down at
some point because we're ultimately just
going to exhaust the productive capacity
of the economy now the productive
capacity of the economy is not a static
you know this you know sort of metric
you know things like Innovation and and
capital deepening can all you know
enhance the productive capacity of the
economy in so much that you know
immigration population growth to do the
same things as well so you know this is
a messy uh uh thing to try to figure out
and in my opinion you know a lot of what
you're trying to figure out uh with
respect to the growth cycle the
inflation cycle the policy cycle both
monetary and fiscal policy and the
liquidity cycle a lot of this is sort of
trying to front run you know the
Market's estimate of whether or not we
are operating well above or well below
potential because ultimately those
things will have significant influences
on you know the AL uh you know
reflexively have influences on the
outlook for policy and the outlook for
quity so uh very complicated stuff here
uh but hopefully um the answer was you
know sort of demystified to to a certain
degree but again there's only a certain
